frameworks.name: thalamocortical loops
frameworks.one_line: Reciprocal thalamus-cortex circuits as substrate for cognitive and sensorimotor function.

frameworks.name: cortico-basal-ganglia-thalamocortical loops
frameworks.one_line: Closed loops through basal ganglia returning via thalamus to cortex.

core_question: What is the comprehensive anatomical connectivity of primate mediodorsal (MD), ventral anterior (VA), and ventrolateral (VL) thalamic nuclei with the cortex?
key_assumptions: Non-sensory thalamic nuclei contribute to cognition and sensorimotor processing via reciprocal cortical loops
Accurate connectivity maps are prerequisite for mechanistic models of thalamic function
Primate connectivity differs enough from rodent that primate-specific synthesis is needed
manipulations: Literature synthesis across tracer studies of MD, VA, VL connectivity in primates
key_findings: MD, VA, and VL each show diverse connections integrating information across multiple brain systems
Connectivity complexity exceeds current standard models
interpretations: Existing models of thalamic contribution to cognition and sensorimotor function are limited by oversimplified connectivity assumptions
Richer connectivity maps motivate new theories of thalamic integration across systems
limitations_noted: Existing primate connectivity data remain limited; further research needed for full characterization
